     </PropertyGroup>

   </Target>
 
   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
Both custom targets have incremental build problems:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — runs every build unconditionally.
- Writes `DateTime.Now` to `build-timestamp.txt`, so the file content **always changes**, which updates the file timestamp and can trigger downstream rebuilds.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **No `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — runs every build unconditionally.
- Calls `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` with `Overwrite="true"` every time, **updating the file timestamp even when the hash hasn't changed**. This forces `CoreCompile` to re-run because a `<Compile>` input changed on disk.

### Root Causes
| Issue | Cause |
|-------|-------|
| Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` | MSBuild can never skip these targets |
| Volatile content (`DateTime.Now`) | Output always differs — breaks any incremental check |
| Unconditional file writes | Even identical content gets a new timestamp |

### Recommended Fixes
- **`GenerateTimestamp`**: Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` or remove the volatile `DateTime.Now`. If you truly need a timestamp, only write it during CI builds (gate with a `Condition`).
- **`EmbedGitHash`**: Add `Inputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` won't work here since inputs=outputs. Instead, use a **two-phase approach**: write to a `.tmp` file, then use `Copy` with `SkipUnchangedFiles="true"` (or a custom diff check) so the real `.g.cs` file is only touched when the hash actually changes.

Would you like me to apply these fixes to the project file?
data.reasoningText:
I can see two custom targets with incremental build issues. Let me analyze them:

1. **`GenerateTimestamp`** - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, and it writes a file containing `DateTime.Now` which changes every build. This will always produce a different file, which would cause downstream targets to re-run.

2. **`EmbedGitHash`** - No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so it runs every build. It writes `git-hash.g.cs` every time, even if the git hash hasn't changed. The `WriteLinesToFile` with `Overwrite="true"` will update the file timestamp even when the content is the same, which would trigger recompilation.
 
The core issue is that both targets lack proper incremental build metadata and unconditionally write files that get compiled, breaking the build cache.
